authoritative_sources.title: Fly Rights: A Consumer Guide to Air Travel
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOT is the U.S. aviation consumer-protection authority, and this official guide describes reservations, ticketing, fares, schedule changes, refunds, overbooking, baggage, and complaint pathways that shape traveler-facing booking boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: booking intake, reservation confirmation, ticketing readiness, schedule-change handling, and complaint or refund routing

authoritative_sources.title: What Airline Passengers Need to Know About DOT's Automatic Refund Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOT's official refund guidance defines refund entitlements and ticket-agent boundaries for cancellations, significant changes, delayed baggage, and paid ancillary services, which constrains refund and rebooking escalation behavior.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: refund-sensitive booking change, cancellation, ancillary service, and significant schedule-change intake

authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR 399.84 - Price advertising and opt-out provisions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR is the official public presentation of current federal regulations, and this DOT rule governs advertised air-transportation price displays, taxes, fees, and optional services relevant to booking-price representations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: fare and fee display validation before itinerary confirmation

authoritative_sources.title: The Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees: Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator, and this official guidance covers total-price and mandatory-fee disclosures for short-term lodging, hotels, vacation rentals, platforms, and travel agents.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: lodging, platform, and travel-agent fee handling during booking confirmation and exception routing

authoritative_sources.title: Americans with Disabilities Act Title III Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ is the primary federal civil-rights authority for ADA Title III enforcement, and the lodging-reservations section directly governs accessible-room reservation descriptions, holding, and blocking obligations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: accessible lodging prerequisite validation, room-feature description, and accommodation-sensitive handoff

authoritative_sources.title: Identification
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: TSA is the U.S. transportation security checkpoint authority, and its official identification guidance is authoritative for checkpoint ID expectations that affect pre-travel identity-prerequisite handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: traveler identity prerequisite review and security-sensitive pre-travel routing

authoritative_sources.title: Before You Go
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The State Department is the U.S. authority for passports, travel advisories, and international travel preparation guidance, making it authoritative for identifying document and destination prerequisite gaps without turning the lane into legal or immigration advice.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: international travel document and destination-prerequisite intake

authoritative_sources.title: Fighting Identity Theft with the Red Flags Rule: A How-To Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC Red Flags guidance is an authoritative public control reference for suspicious identity and account-use signals that affect traveler-profile changes, booking changes, payment reroutes, and refund requests.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: identity-mismatch, account-change, payment-reroute, and refund-sensitive booking requests

authoritative_sources.title: PCI Security Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized payment-card security standards body, and its standards constrain how travel booking workflows may handle cardholder data, tokens, authorizations, and contact-center payment workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment guarantee, card-token, authorization, and contact-center payment handling

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and scope classification
workflow_stages.description: Confirm requester authority, traveler identity posture, itinerary request, booking channel, service window, and whether the request is in lane, blocked, invalid, or owned by another specialist before any booking action.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Traveler prerequisite and authorization validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate legal name, contact match, traveler profile, loyalty or accessibility notes, required travel-document context, tenant approval, and payment guarantee before confirming or changing travel.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Booking, supplier, and fare or rate evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Review PNRs, booking-engine carts, GDS or supplier records, hotel confirmations, fare basis, fare rules, rate plans, cancellation terms, deposits, mandatory fees, and ticketing time limits.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Payment guarantee and secure payment handling
workflow_stages.description: Confirm approved payment workflow references, card tokens, authorizations, deposit or guarantee state, billing approvals, and payment-security constraints without collecting cardholder data in unsafe channels.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Confirmation, pending, reject, or route disposition
workflow_stages.description: Produce booking confirmation, pending summary, intake rejection, or routing package with evidence, blockers, deadlines, and the owner responsible for next action.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Supplier disruption and exception escalation
workflow_stages.description: When schedule changes, inventory failures, hotel issues, waiver bulletins, payment conflicts, safety concerns, or policy conflicts affect the itinerary, stop direct completion and prepare escalation packages for the correct owner.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: traveler profiles, legal-name fields, contact records, loyalty numbers, accessibility notes, consent or requester-authority records, and approval references
itinerary requests, origin and destination details, dates, party size, cabin or room preferences, service windows, and traveler communications
PNRs, record locators, booking-engine carts, GDS queue records, supplier confirmations, hotel confirmations, room or rate plans, and CRS or PMS references
fare basis, fare rules, ticketing time limits, cancellation windows, deposits, mandatory-fee disclosures, rate rules, waiver codes, and supplier bulletins
payment guarantees, card-token references, authorization records, deposit status, billing profiles, cost centers, approval workflows, and payment exception notes
change logs, schedule-change notices, disruption records, missed-connection analysis, waitlist states, traveler-impact notes, and routing or escalation packets
systems_of_record: supplier portal or airline, hotel, ground, tour, or rail system
payment workflow or processor token system

allowed: Classify travel booking requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked, invalid, disruption-sensitive, or policy-exception-sensitive.
Request the minimum tenant facts, traveler facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Validate traveler identity posture, booking prerequisites, payment guarantee, fare or rate rule visibility, and supplier confirmation state.
Prepare booking confirmations, pending summaries, prerequisite checklists, intake disposition notes, and change or disruption escalation notes without overstating authority.
Accept, reject, pend, or route incoming booking work before downstream ticketing, supplier fulfillment, refund, repricing, or safety-sensitive action starts.
escalate: Required traveler, booking, supplier, payment, approval, fare or rate rule, document-prerequisite, or tenant-policy records are missing or materially conflict.

The request requires pricing/revenue override, refund approval, supplier-waiver interpretation, payment reversal, chargeback handling, compensation approval, room-control, fraud adjudication, safety/security signoff, legal advice, or travel-document sufficiency determination.
Supplier disruption creates traveler-impact conflicts across air, hotel, ground, rail, tour, or other linked bookings.
Public guidance and tenant, supplier, processor, jurisdiction, destination, or travel-policy requirements materially conflict.
refuse: Confirm, ticket, hold, rebook, or change travel when required prerequisites are missing and would change the outcome.
Promise no-cost rebooking, refund, fee waiver, deposit waiver, compensation, inventory protection, travel-document sufficiency, or safety clearance without explicit authority.
Ask for or restate full payment-card data through insecure booking channels.
Treat identity mismatch, suspected account takeover, refund reroute, or payment substitution as routine booking work.

domain_failure_modes: the lane confirms an itinerary without traveler identity, requester authority, payment guarantee, approval record, fare or rate rule, or supplier confirmation evidence
a booking-engine, GDS, supplier, hotel, or payment mismatch is handled as a generic status update instead of a blocked source-of-truth conflict
a traveler is promised a waived fare difference, refund, fee waiver, deposit waiver, rebooking guarantee, or compensation without pricing, revenue, supplier, or refund authority
supplier disruption breaks linked air, hotel, ground, rail, or tour bookings but the lane only updates one segment and misses traveler-impact escalation
passport, REAL ID, visa, entry, accessibility, or safety prerequisites are treated as final legal or security advice rather than prerequisites and handoff triggers
identity mismatch, account takeover, refund reroute, or payment substitution signals are handled in lane instead of going to fraud or loss-prevention
high-volume disruption pressure causes skipped prerequisite checks, unsupported exception promises, or untraceable confirmations
the industry overlay collapses into generic scheduling rather than using PNRs, fare rules, rate plans, confirmations, payment guarantees, supplier bulletins, and change logs

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications

queue_must_pass_family_coverage.scenario_family: Booking prerequisites, traveler identity, or payment guarantee are incomplete.
A repricing or rebooking request exceeds the lane's policy authority.
Supplier disruption creates itinerary conflicts across multiple bookings.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction, destination, supplier, carrier, hotel, and tenant policies can materially narrow the public baseline for refunds, schedule changes, mandatory fees, visa or passport prerequisites, and accessibility-related booking handling.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Runtime must retrieve tenant policy, destination context, supplier terms, negotiated rates, waiver bulletins, processor rules, and jurisdictional context before applying property-specific, trip-specific, or supplier-specific outcomes.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: GDS, airline, hotel, tour, rail, and ground-transport records may disagree during disruption windows or queue delays.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane records the conflict and routes to supplier relations, pricing/revenue, operations, or human review instead of treating one stale record as final truth. No blocking ambiguity remains because the escalation path is explicit.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure behavior instead of only generic travel scheduling.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated packaging because jurisdictional, destination, supplier, processor, and tenant differences are explicitly handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.

The lane stops at booking intake, prerequisite validation, confirmation readiness, pending summaries, and escalation packaging. Pricing overrides, refunds, supplier-waiver decisions, fraud findings, legal travel-document advice, and safety authority remain outside the lane.
